amr.heading: What is AMR?
amr.description: Antimicrobial Resistance occurs when bacteria evolve to resist the medicines designed to kill them. Improper disposal of antibiotics directly fuels this crisis.

authDesc: Turn your unused medicines into real benefits — protect your health, your environment, and your wallet. Get rewarded every time you act responsibly.
whySafe1: Protect soil and water from harmful contamination
whySafe3: Keep your home safer for children
findPharmLocations: Locate trusted pharmacies around you in seconds
earnDesc: Up to 20% savings — redeem at partner pharmacies
trackDesc: Hand over medicines safely and unlock exclusive deals

impact.heroDesc: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) occurs when bacteria evolve to resist the medicines designed to kill them. Common infections become untreatable, and routine treatments fail.
impact.crisisHighlight: Maharashtra is formally recognised as one of India's highest AMR-risk states.
impact.crisisBullet1: Maharashtra is one of only nine states in India with a formally established State AMR Surveillance Network.
impact.sourceNcdc: Source: NCDC India — National AMR Surveillance Network
impact.crisisBullet2: P.D. Hinduja Hospital Mumbai and hospitals in Pune are part of ICMR's national AMR Surveillance Network (IAMRSN) — selected due to the severity of resistance patterns recorded in the region.
impact.klebDesc: Susceptibility to Imipenem (a last-resort antibiotic) dropped from 65% in 2016 to just 43% in 2021 across ICMR network hospitals including those in Maharashtra's western zone.
impact.ecoliDesc: Susceptibility to Imipenem dropped from 86% in 2016 to 64% in 2021. Carbapenem-resistant E. coli strains were also resistant to most other antibiotics, leaving doctors with almost no options.
impact.vectorDesc: Significant monthly increases in carbapenem resistance for Klebsiella, E. coli, and Acinetobacter were recorded across ICMR hospitals in Maharashtra's western zone (2017-2022).
impact.sourceLancet: Source: The Lancet Regional Health — SE Asia (May 2024)
impact.advResDesc: P.D. Hinduja Hospital Mumbai is one of six hospitals selected nationwide for a landmark ICMR study on carbapenem-resistant infections — the most dangerous category of bacteria.
impact.sourceBioSpectrum: Source: BioSpectrum India / ICMR SBI-CREP-01 Study
impact.vul1: Maharashtra has the highest urban density in India outside Delhi — overcrowded hospitals accelerate the spread of resistant strains.
impact.vul2: Antibiotics like Azithromycin, Ciprofloxacin, and Amoxicillin are routinely available over the counter in Maharashtra without prescriptions.
impact.vul3: Fluoroquinolone resistance in India peaked at 94% in 2017, with Maharashtra's high antibiotic consumption heavily steering national trends.
impact.dispDesc: When unused antibiotics are thrown in the trash or flushed into sewage, their chemical compounds enter water systems and soil — creating low-level antibiotic exposure that trains environmental bacteria to become resistant.
impact.disp1: India has no organised citizen-facing system for safe medicine disposal. Maharashtra, despite being India's most industrialised state, is no exception.
impact.disp2: The misuse and improper disposal of antimicrobials in both humans and animals is identified as the primary driver of AMR by ICMR and WHO.
impact.sourceIcmr2022: Source: ICMR AMR Surveillance Annual Report 2022
impact.means1: Every unused antibiotic strip left in a drawer, thrown in the bin, or flushed down the drain is a direct contribution to Maharashtra's growing AMR crisis.
impact.means2: AMRit gives every citizen in Maharashtra a simple, rewarding way to break this cycle — dispose safely, earn points, and protect the next generation from untreatable infections.

guestDialog.title: Get Rewards!
guestDialog.description: To schedule a disposal and earn reward points, please create an account or log in.

onboarding.step1Desc: Click the 'Schedule Safe Disposal' button to begin the process of responsibly clearing out your unused medicines.
onboarding.step2Desc: Search for the medicine name and fill in the details. This helps us ensure they are disposed of correctly.
onboarding.step3Desc: Review your disposal package and click 'Review Package' to finalize your request.
onboarding.step4Desc: Now that you've reviewed your package, you can finish your first disposal. This will clear the onboarding process.
